claim_bullets.bullet_text: One is that while functions like take(...) appear to create an entirely new iterator, in reality they return a decorated reference to the original iterator (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.176-176).
Please note that unlike most of the other functions discussed in this book, iterators are stateful (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.176-176).
So as you traverse the new decorator, you're changing the state of the original! (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.176-176).

source_record_text: This section discusses the stateful nature of iterators and important implications of stateful functions in JavaScript Allongé.
